title: Parallel Tool Calls, TaskGroup Ownership, Ordering, and Shared Effects · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved agent trajectory contains one plausible model, schema, tool, authority, state, loop, memory, concurrency, injection, evaluation, telemetry, deployment, or recovery defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in structured bounded parallel tool executor and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Prove tool-call independence from inputs, effects, authority, ordering, rate limits, shared resources, and compensating behavior before parallel execution.
Own concurrent calls with asyncio TaskGroup, explicit timeout and semaphore budgets, and structured error and cancellation propagation.
Correlate each result by call ID and return required result parts in provider-valid order independent of completion order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Call Independence
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug agent engineering team handles case 8110 during an accessible public-service intake assistant. Build deterministic Python evidence to run independent tools with bounded TaskGroup ownership, stable call-result correlation, serialized shared effects, cancellation, and cleanup; sensitive actions require a trustworthy user approval bound to exact arguments; then replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Parallel Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-parallel-cleanup-18gcug" that uses case 9562 to run independent tools with bounded TaskGroup ownership, stable call-result correlation, serialized shared effects, cancellation, and cleanup. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Parallel Cleanup.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Parallel Cleanup into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Parallel Cleanup, keep Parallel Cleanup connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-parallel-cleanup-18gcug
# Competency: agentpy-parallel-cleanup.
# Case 9562: an accessible public-service intake assistant.
# Operating constraint: last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure.
# Changed case: introduce a sensitive action, poisoned memory, leaking log, or computer-use request and deny or pause it.
def evidence_agentpy_parallel_cleanup_18gcug(scheduled_calls: int = 12, completed_calls: int = 12, parallel_limit: int = 4, peak_parallel: int = 3, leaked_tasks: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_18gcug = "18gcug-gucg81-9562"
    assert evidence_variant_18gcug
    evidence_axes_18gcug = (
        "authority18gcug",
        "interaction9562",
        "toolgucg81",
        "state18gcug9562",
        "evaluation956218gcug",
        "recoverygucg819562",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_18gcug)) == 6
    if scheduled_calls < 0 or completed_calls < 0:
        return False, completed_calls, "parallel-count"
    if completed_calls != scheduled_calls:
        return False, completed_calls, "parallel-reconciliation"
    if parallel_limit < 1 or peak_parallel > parallel_limit:
        return False, peak_parallel, "parallel-budget"
    if leaked_tasks > 0:
        return False, leaked_tasks, "task-leak"
    assert completed_calls == scheduled_calls and leaked_tasks == 0
    return True, completed_calls, "structured-tool-concurrency"
